 {"id":"bd-1mdym","title":"Task: Implement --robot-ensemble-modes and --robot-ensemble-presets","description":"# Task: Implement --robot-ensemble-modes and --robot-ensemble-presets

Implement robot commands for listing available modes and ensembles.

## Command Specification
```bash
ntm --robot-ensemble-modes [--category CATEGORY] [--tier core|advanced|experimental|all] [--limit N] [--offset N]
ntm --robot-ensemble-presets
```

- Modifiers are **unprefixed** per robot-api-design.md (`--category`, `--tier`, `--limit`, `--offset`).
- `--category` accepts code (A-L) or name (Formal, Ampliative, ...).

## Output Schema

### Full Mode List (default = core tier only)
```json
{
  "action": "ensemble_modes",
  "modes": [
    {
      "id": "deductive",
      "code": "A1",
      "tier": "core",
      "name": "Deductive Inference",
      "category": {"code": "A", "name": "Formal"},
      "short_desc": "Classical logic reasoning",
      "description": "...",
      "outputs": "Valid/invalid argument classification",
      "best_for": ["Contract validation"],
      "differentiator": "Unlike induction (B1)...",
      "failure_modes": ["Hidden premises"],
      "icon": "⊢",
      "color": "#A1A1A1"
    }
  ],
  "categories": [
    {"code": "A", "name": "Formal", "mode_count": 8}
  ],
  "default_tier": "core",
  "total_modes": 80,
  "core_modes": 28,
  "advanced_modes": 52,
  "experimental_modes": 0
}
```

### Category + Tier Filter
```bash
ntm --robot-ensemble-modes --category Formal --tier advanced
```

### Presets List
```json
{
  "action": "ensemble_presets",
  "presets": [
    {
      "name": "project-diagnosis",
      "display": "Project Diagnosis",
      "description": "Comprehensive project health analysis",
      "modes": ["systems-thinking", "failure-mode", "perspective-taking"],
      "synthesis": {"strategy": "adversarial"},
      "budget": {"total": 30000},
      "allow_advanced": false
    }
  ]
}
```

## Implementation
```go
// internal/robot/ensemble_modes.go
func HandleEnsembleModes(category, tier string, limit, offset int) (*EnsembleModesResponse, error)
func filterByCategory(modes []Mode, category string) []Mode
func filterByTier(modes []Mode, tier string) []Mode
func buildCategorySummary(modes []Mode) []CategoryInfo
```

### File Location
internal/robot/ensemble_modes.go
internal/robot/ensemble_presets.go

### Dependencies
- Requires mode catalog (bd-qn9gc)
- Tier metadata support (bd-3806s)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Default output only includes core tier
- [ ] Tier filter works (core/advanced/experimental/all)
- [ ] Category filter accepts code or name
- [ ] JSON includes tier + counts (incl. experimental)
- [ ] RobotResponse envelope compliance
- [ ] Arrays initialized to [] when empty","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:40:59.724643849Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:50:29.572177836Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:50:29.572100713Z","close_reason":"Implemented robot API for ensemble modes (--robot-ensemble-modes) and presets (--robot-ensemble-presets) with full test coverage","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mdym","depends_on_id":"bd-1ovi8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mdym","depends_on_id":"bd-3806s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mdym","depends_on_id":"bd-qn9gc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1mlei","title":"Feature: ACFS setup status integration","description":"## Overview
Expose ACFS (flywheel setup) status in NTM to help agents detect missing prerequisites.

## Robot Mode API

```
ntm --robot-acfs-status
```

**Naming Pattern**: Follows tool bridge convention `--robot-<tool>-<action>`

Note: Also aliased as `ntm --robot-setup` for discoverability.

## JSON Output Schema
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "acfs_version": "1.2.3",
  "tools": {
    "br": {"installed": true, "version": "0.8.0", "path": "/usr/local/bin/br"},
    "cc": {"installed": true, "version": "1.0.0", "path": "/usr/local/bin/claude"},
    "cod": {"installed": false, "hint": "npm install -g @openai/codex-cli"}
  }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- NTM can report ACFS presence and version
- Shows which core tools are installed with versions
- Missing tools surface actionable hints","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-1mn8p","title":"Task: DCG (Destructive Command Guard) adapter + robot status","description":"## Overview\nIntegrate Destructive Command Guard (dcg) as a first-class tool.\n\n## Deliverables\n- Add tools.ToolDCG + adapter (detect/version/capabilities/health)\n- Doctor shows dcg status and whether shell guard is active\n- Robot tools report includes dcg\n- Optional: surface dcg status in dashboard (non-blocking)\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- No hard dependency if dcg not installed\n- Health check uses 'dcg status --json' if available","notes":"## Testing Requirements

 {"id":"bd-1mnb9","title":"Task: Unit tests for Local LLM Support","description":"## Overview
Comprehensive unit tests for the Ollama/local LLM integration.

## Test Coverage Required

### 1. Ollama Adapter Tests (ollama_adapter_test.go)
- TestConnect: Successfully connects to Ollama server
- TestConnect_Unavailable: Handles connection failure gracefully
- TestSendPrompt: Sends prompt and receives response
- TestSendPrompt_Timeout: Handles timeout correctly
- TestStreamResponse: Streaming tokens work
- TestStreamResponse_Cancel: Context cancellation works
- TestListModels: Returns available models
- TestPullModel: Initiates model download
- TestPullModel_Progress: Progress callbacks work

### 2. Mock Ollama Server
```go
type MockOllamaServer struct {
    models    []string
    responses map[string]string
    latency   time.Duration
}

func (m *MockOllamaServer) HandleGenerate(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    // Simulate streaming response
}
```

### 3. Spawn Integration Tests (spawn_local_test.go)
- TestSpawnLocal_FlagParsing: --local=N parsed correctly
- TestSpawnLocal_ModelFlag: --local-model=X works
- TestSpawnLocal_HostFlag: --local-host=X works
- TestSpawnLocal_MixedFleet: --cc=2 --local=2 works
- TestSpawnLocal_Validation: Invalid model handled
- TestSpawnLocal_OllamaDown: Graceful error when unavailable

### 4. Performance Metrics Tests (metrics_test.go)
- TestTokensPerSecond: Calculation correct
- TestTokensPerSecond_Window: Sliding window works
- TestMemoryUsage: Parses Ollama /api/ps response
- TestLatency: First-token latency tracked
- TestMetricsAggregation: Metrics combine correctly

### 5. Model Management Tests (models_test.go)
- TestListModels: Lists cached models
- TestListModels_Empty: Handles no models
- TestPullModel_Success: Downloads model
- TestPullModel_AlreadyExists: Skips existing
- TestRemoveModel: Deletes model
- TestRecommendModel: Suggests based on VRAM
- TestRecommendModel_NoGPU: CPU-only recommendations

## Test Fixtures
```go
var mockOllamaResponse = `{"model":"codellama:7b","created_at":"2025-01-15T10:00:00Z","response":"func main() {","done":false}
{"model":"codellama:7b","created_at":"2025-01-15T10:00:00Z","response":"\n","done":false}
{"model":"codellama:7b","created_at":"2025-01-15T10:00:00Z","response":"","done":true,"total_duration":1234567890}`
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- 85%+ code coverage
- Mock server enables offline testing
- All tests pass without real Ollama
- Tests run in <10 seconds","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:21:20.579646406Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:27:07.571527098Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mnb9","depends_on_id":"bd-1t8p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mnb9","depends_on_id":"bd-1tb8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mnb9","depends_on_id":"bd-852e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mnb9","depends_on_id":"bd-a7wm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mnb9","depends_on_id":"bd-zcft","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1mxyl","title":"DCG: Add dashboard indicator for DCG status","description":"Add visual indicator in NTM dashboard showing DCG protection status.

## Design

### Location
Top-right status area of dashboard, alongside other status indicators.

### Visual States
- 🛡️ DCG (green): DCG active and protecting
- ⚠️ DCG (yellow): DCG enabled but binary not found
- (none): DCG disabled in config

### Tooltip/Details
When selected, show:
- Commands checked this session
- Commands blocked this session
- Last blocked command (if any)

## Implementation

### Dashboard Model Update
```go
type DashboardModel struct {
    // ... existing fields
    DCGStatus DCGStatusModel
}

type DCGStatusModel struct {
    Enabled   bool
    Available bool
    Checked   int64
    Blocked   int64
    LastBlock *BlockedCommand
}
```

### View Update
Add to dashboard header/status bar:
```go
func (m DashboardModel) View() string {
    // ... existing view code
    dcgIndicator := m.renderDCGIndicator()
    // Insert into status bar
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/tui/dashboard/dcg_indicator.go
- Update: internal/tui/dashboard/model.go

## Styling
Use lipgloss for consistent styling with rest of dashboard","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:45:23.254864594Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:20:40.472504211Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:20:40.472457132Z","close_reason":"Implemented DCG status indicator badge in dashboard stats bar","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mxyl","depends_on_id":"bd-1m6sw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mxyl","depends_on_id":"bd-2q9px","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mxyl","depends_on_id":"bd-o97j9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
@@ -345,6 +345,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2g6y","title":"Add NO_COLOR and TERM=dumb support for accessible plain mode","description":"## Problem
Plain theme may still show colors; need full NO_COLOR compliance.

## Code Review Findings (2026-01-19)

### Good News: NO_COLOR Already Implemented

**theme.go:278-299** already handles NO_COLOR:
```go
// Respects the NO_COLOR standard (https://no-color.org/):
// - If NO_COLOR exists in environment (any value), colors are disabled
// - NTM_NO_COLOR=1 also disables colors
// - NTM_NO_COLOR=0 forces colors ON (overrides NO_COLOR)
func NoColorEnabled() bool {
    ntmNoColor := strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv("NTM_NO_COLOR"))
    ...
    _, noColorSet := os.LookupEnv("NO_COLOR")
    ...
}
```

**output/*.go** files also check NO_COLOR:
- text.go:189
- success.go:43
- errors.go:58
- confirm.go:53
- progress.go:56,201

### Missing: TERM=dumb Support

No handling found for TERM=dumb. This should be added:
```go
func NoColorEnabled() bool {
    // Existing NO_COLOR checks...

    // Add TERM=dumb check
    if os.Getenv("TERM") == "dumb" {
        return true
    }

    return noColorSet
}
```

### Missing: ASCII Fallback Verification

Need to verify Plain theme uses ASCII-only characters:
- Box drawing: +-| instead of Unicode
- Status icons: [OK] [FAIL] instead of ✓ ✗

## Fix Implementation

### 1. Add TERM=dumb Check
```go
// theme.go
func NoColorEnabled() bool {
    // ... existing checks ...

    // TERM=dumb should also disable colors
    if os.Getenv("TERM") == "dumb" {
        return true
    }

    return noColorSet
}
```

### 2. Verify ASCII Icon Fallback
```go
// icons/icons.go
func asciiIcons() IconSet {
    return IconSet{
        Success: "[OK]",
        Error:   "[ERR]",
        Warning: "[WARN]",
        Info:    "[INFO]",
        // ... all ASCII
    }
}
```

## Unit Tests Required
```go
func TestTermDumbDisablesColor(t *testing.T) {
    t.Setenv("TERM", "dumb")
    t.Setenv("NTM_NO_COLOR", "")

    if !NoColorEnabled() {
        t.Error("TERM=dumb should enable no-color mode")
    }
}

func TestPlainThemeNoANSI(t *testing.T) {
    theme.SetCurrent(theme.Plain)

    styles := NewSemanticStyles(theme.Current())
    rendered := styles.TextSuccess.Render("test")

    if strings.Contains(rendered, "\x1b[") {
        t.Error("Plain theme should not contain ANSI escape codes")
    }
}

func TestPlainThemeASCIIOnly(t *testing.T) {
    theme.SetCurrent(theme.Plain)
    icons := icons.Current()

    // All icons should be ASCII
    for name, icon := range icons.All() {
        for _, r := range icon {
            if r > 127 {
                t.Errorf("icon %s contains non-ASCII character: %c", name, r)
            }
        }
    }
}
```

## Verification
- [x] NO_COLOR=1 handled
- [x] NTM_NO_COLOR handled
- [ ] TERM=dumb handling - needs implementation
- [ ] ASCII-only verification for Plain theme","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:13:44.377695088Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:18:01.993995475Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:18:01.993945761Z","close_reason":"Added TERM=dumb support to NoColorEnabled() function. When TERM=dumb is set, colors are disabled (returns true from NoColorEnabled). NTM_NO_COLOR=0 can still override to force colors on. Added unit tests for both TERM=dumb enabling no-color mode and NTM_NO_COLOR=0 overriding TERM=dumb. Updated docstring. Verified ASCII fallback already exists in icons.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2g6y","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2gnp3","title":"rano: Implement --robot-rano-stats command","description":"## Overview

Add `ntm --robot-rano-stats` command for per-agent network statistics via rano integration.

## IMPORTANT: API Design Consistency

This command follows the established robot mode patterns:
- **Naming**: `--robot-rano-stats` (tool prefix: rano)
- **NOT** `--robot-network-stats` (must include tool name)
- **Modifiers**: shared `--panes=` flag

## Command Interface

```bash
# Get network stats for all agents
ntm --robot-rano-stats

# Filter to specific pane
ntm --robot-rano-stats --panes=2,3

# Custom time window
ntm --robot-rano-stats --rano-window=10m
```

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "window": "5m",
  "agents": {
    "pane_2": {
      "connections": 3,
      "bytes_sent": 45000,
      "bytes_received": 120000,
      "requests_estimate": 15,
      "providers": {
        "anthropic": {
          "connections": 2,
          "bytes_sent": 40000,
          "bytes_received": 100000
        }
      },
      "avg_latency_ms": 250
    }
  },
  "total": {
    "connections": 4,
    "bytes_sent": 55000,
    "bytes_received": 170000
  }
}
```

## Error Response

```json
{
  "success": false,
  "error": "rano not found in PATH",
  "hint": "Install rano: go install github.com/.../rano@latest"
}
```

## Implementation

1. Check rano availability via ToolRegistry
2. Get PID map for all/specified panes via tmux
3. Call rano with PIDs: `rano --pids=PID1,PID2 --window=DURATION --json`
4. Aggregate stats by pane
5. Format as JSON

## Modifier Flags

| Flag | Description | Default |
|------|-------------|---------|
| `--panes=N` | Filter to specific pane(s) | All agent panes |
| `--rano-window=DURATION` | Time window for stats | 5m |

## Code Location
- internal/robot/rano_stats.go

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `--robot-rano-stats` flag registered in root.go
- [ ] `--panes=N` filter works with comma-separated pane IDs
- [ ] `--rano-window=DURATION` accepts Go duration format (5m, 1h, etc.)
- [ ] JSON output matches schema exactly
- [ ] Missing rano returns actionable error with install hint
- [ ] PID-to-pane mapping correctly attributes traffic
- [ ] Provider breakdown (anthropic/openai/google) works
- [ ] Exit code 0 on success, 1 on partial data, 2 on error

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (internal/robot/rano_stats_test.go)
- [ ] Flag parsing for all options
- [ ] PID-to-pane mapping logic
- [ ] rano output parsing
- [ ] Provider aggregation
- [ ] JSON output serialization
- [ ] Error handling for missing rano
- [ ] Error handling for rano failures

### E2E Tests (scripts/e2e_robot_rano.sh)
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
# [E2E-RANO] Robot Rano Stats Tests
set -euo pipefail
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-RANO]"

echo "$LOG_PREFIX Starting robot rano E2E tests"

# Check if rano is available
if \! command -v rano &>/dev/null; then
    echo "$LOG_PREFIX SKIP: rano not installed"
    exit 0
fi

# Setup: Create test session
TEST_SESSION="e2e_rano_$$"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Creating test session: $TEST_SESSION"
ntm spawn "$TEST_SESSION" --cc=1 --no-prompt
sleep 5

# Test 1: Basic stats
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Test 1: Basic rano stats"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-rano-stats)
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Result: $RESULT"
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r ".success") == "true" ]] || exit 1
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r ".timestamp") \!= "" ]] || exit 1

# Test 2: Pane filter
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Test 2: Pane-filtered stats"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-rano-stats --panes=1)
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Result: $RESULT"
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r ".success") == "true" ]] || exit 1

# Test 3: Custom window
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Test 3: Custom time window"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-rano-stats --rano-window=1m)
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Result: $RESULT"
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r ".window") == "1m" ]] || exit 1

# Cleanup
tmux kill-session -t "$TEST_SESSION" 2>/dev/null || true

echo "$LOG_PREFIX All robot rano E2E tests passed"

 {"id":"bd-2gp6","title":"Fix ANSI escape code width calculation errors","description":"## Problem
Width calculations not properly accounting for ANSI escape codes, causing misalignment and overflow.

## Code Review Findings (2026-01-19)

**Good news: The codebase already handles ANSI width correctly in most places.**

### Already Correct:
- `internal/cli/table.go:208` - `runeWidth()` uses `lipgloss.Width(s)`
- `internal/tui/layout/layout.go:102` - `TruncateWidth()` uses `lipgloss.Width()`
- `internal/tui/styles/badges.go:687` - Documents: "Uses lipgloss.Width() to properly handle ANSI codes"
- `internal/tui/dashboard/panels/ticker.go` - Builds plain text first, then styles visible portion
- 36+ uses of `lipgloss.Width()` found across the codebase

### Remaining Audit Needed
Verify no edge cases where `len()` is used on strings that might already be styled:
1. Dashboard panel rendering code
2. Card grid layout calculations  
3. Status line construction
4. Any dynamic width calculations based on content

## Updated Fix Approach
1. **Audit** all width calculations - verify they use lipgloss.Width() or operate on plain text
2. **Add tests** to verify styled string width handling
3. **Document** the correct pattern in CONTRIBUTING.md

## Unit Tests Required
```go
func TestANSIWidthCalculations(t *testing.T) {
    // Verify lipgloss.Width() correctly ignores ANSI codes
    plain := "Hello"
    styled := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("212")).Render("Hello")
    
    if lipgloss.Width(styled) != lipgloss.Width(plain) {
        t.Errorf("styled width %d != plain width %d", lipgloss.Width(styled), lipgloss.Width(plain))
    }
    
    // Verify runeWidth() uses lipgloss.Width()
    w := runeWidth(styled)
    if w != 5 {
        t.Errorf("runeWidth(styled) = %d, want 5", w)
    }
}

func TestGradientTextWidth(t *testing.T) {
    text := "Test"
    gradient := styles.GradientText(text, "#FF0000", "#00FF00")
    
    // Gradient text should have same display width as original
    if lipgloss.Width(gradient) != 4 {
        t.Errorf("gradient width = %d, want 4", lipgloss.Width(gradient))
    }
}

func TestTableColumnAlignment(t *testing.T) {
    table := NewStyledTable("Name", "Value")
    // Add rows with styled content
    table.AddRow(lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Render("Bold"), "plain")
    table.AddRow("plain", lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("212")).Render("colored"))
    
    rendered := table.Render()
    lines := strings.Split(rendered, "\n")
    
    // All lines should have consistent width (no jagged edges)
    var widths []int
    for _, line := range lines {
        if strings.TrimSpace(line) != "" {
            widths = append(widths, lipgloss.Width(line))
        }
    }
    
    // Check all widths are equal (within tolerance for borders)
    for i := 1; i < len(widths); i++ {
        if widths[i] != widths[0] {
            t.Errorf("line %d width %d != first line width %d", i, widths[i], widths[0])
        }
    }
}
```

## Verification
- Run audit to confirm no `len()` used on styled strings for width
- All styled text aligns correctly in tables
- Gradient text does not cause overflow
- Unit tests pass

## Priority Adjustment
Changed from P1 to P2 - most code is already correct, this is mainly an audit/verification task.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:09:28.352073676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T08:59:12.711902846Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T08:59:12.711841760Z","close_reason":"Audit complete: no len() on styled strings found.
